Fun game but no one plays
This make you feel like Gods fighting.

Fans of Blaston should love this one! It's a similar two person duel type game with a bit more strategy and a mythical vibe. It's a strategy-based VR game where you play as a deity, controlling a champion in battle. It's like stepping into a mythological chess game where you're both the player and a piece on the board.

The graphics are quite good. It makes you feel like you're in some ancient, epic world. Gameplay-wise, Divine Duel strikes a nice balance between strategy and action. You're not just passively watching; you're actively making decisions, casting spells, and shifting the tide of battle. It's this awesome mix of thinking on your feet while also planning your next move.

Multiplayer is where this game shines. Going head-to-head against other players, testing your strategy, and adapting to their moves is super engaging. It's like a VR chess match with divine powers – totally immersive.

The beginning tutorial was great in explaining how the game works. The controls are intuitive, which is a big plus in VR. I felt I could get started relatively quickly without having to spend too much time figuring out what to do, which is great!

It's also an excellent workout. The time flew buy and I was really worn out after this gameplay. I will definitely be playing more Divine Duel and suggest you give it a try!
An absolute amazing game overall just the graphics the gameplay everything is just perfection and when this game gets out of app lab it is going to be one of the greatest

Very fun and engaging game. Weapons are varied and allow for varitety of playstyles. Presentation is also quite good and every celestial feels unique. It's also a good  game for working out because defeating your fellow men is a great motivator. Overall definetly worth a try.
10/10 beautiful graphics and tons of play time always getting better
Super fun 1v1 game i recommend this game for everyone

I haven't gotten a game that I actually wanna play constantly on vr quite yet until I found this absolute one in a million, it pretty much offers most of what I personally expected in vr which I'm quite new to.
